title: Pentacalia tablensis
intro: Pentacalia tablensis is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, commonly found in the Andean region of South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wound healing, fever reduction, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia tablensis include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used in folk medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to promote digestive health.
taxonomy: Pentacalia tablensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Pentacalia, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as the "five-leaved pentacalias". The genus Pentacalia is a part of the tribe Senecioneae, a large and diverse group of daisy-like plants. The tribe Senecioneae is further classified within the subfamily Cichorioideae, a subfamily of the Asteraceae family. Pentacalia tablensis is native to Ecuador, where it can be found growing in the wild.
